message:
🌱 Add parsec: Memory cleanup of sensitive data mission (#970)

Signed-off-by: github-actions[bot] <41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>
Co-authored-by: github-actions[bot] <41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,71 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "parsec-122-memory-cleanup-of-sensitive-data",
+  "missionClass": "solution",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "parsec: Memory cleanup of sensitive data",
+    "description": "Memory cleanup of sensitive data. Community-reported issue.",
+    "type": "troubleshoot",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Identify parsec setup",
+        "description": "Check your Parsec service installation:\n```bash\nparsec-tool ping\n```\nParsec is a Platform AbstRaction for SECurity service. This issue is about ensuring sensitive data (keys, secrets) is properly zeroed from memory after use."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Review the security concern",
+        "description": "From the PSA Crypto API version 1.0.0, section 6.3.3:\n\n> Implementations must wipe all sensitive data from memory when it is no longer used. It is recommended that they wipe this sensitive data as soon as possible.\n\nThe Parsec service handles cryptographic keys and secrets. Memory cleanup of sensitive data prevents data leakage through memory reuse."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix for memory cleanup of sensitive data",
+        "description": "The fix involves using the `zeroize` crate to wipe sensitive values from memory when they are no longer needed. Key changes:\n1. Add the `zeroize` crate as a dependency\n2. Implement `Zeroize` for types that hold sensitive data (keys, secrets)\n3. Use `Zeroizing<T>` wrappers or explicit `zeroize()` calls when values go out of scope\n\nSee the [source issue](https://github.com/parallaxsecond/parsec/issues/122) for community-verified solutions and implementation details."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify the fix works",
+        "description": "Confirm that Parsec still functions correctly after the memory cleanup changes:\n```bash\nparsec-tool ping\nparsec-tool list-keys\n```\nVerify that cryptographic operations complete successfully and that sensitive data is no longer retained in memory after use."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "Resolved by integrating the zeroize crate to ensure sensitive cryptographic data (keys, secrets) is wiped from memory as soon as it is no longer needed, following PSA Crypto API section 6.3.3 guidelines.",
+      "codeSnippets": []
+    }
+  },
+  "metadata": {
+    "tags": [
+      "parsec",
+      "sandbox",
+      "app-definition",
+      "troubleshoot"
+    ],
+    "cncfProjects": [

+    ],

+    ],

+    },
+    "reactions": 1,
+    "comments": 13,
+    "synthesizedBy": "copilot"
+  },

+    "tools": [
+      "parsec-tool"
+    ],

+  },
